governance_findings.description: Canonical CSR and SDC candidate CSR use different domain numbering. Canonical DOMAIN-01='Edge Data Acquisition' but SDC DOMAIN-01='Fleet Core Operations'. The proposition bridge matched by domain_id, creating false component count cross-references in domain grounding propositions.
governance_findings.governance_action: SP-0017 REJECTED (wrong domain mapping). SP-0016 CONTESTED then ACCEPTED with reduced confidence. SP-0038 REJECTED (61 components from wrong SDC domain).
governance_findings.remediation: proposition_bridge.py should match domains by semantic name, not by ID. Requires re-derivation in RC-04 evidence enrichment.

governance_findings.description: SDC extracted HTML layer labels as capability names: 'Layer 4 — NestJS Application', 'Layer 6 — Frontend Applicat' (truncated), 'Unknown Layer'. These are document structure artifacts, not semantic capabilities.
governance_findings.governance_action: All 3 REJECTED. Layer labels are not capabilities.
governance_findings.remediation: SDC section extraction should filter layer headers from capability derivation.

governance_findings.description: SDC derived 19 domains but canonical CSR has 17. DOMAIN-18 (Data Storage) and DOMAIN-19 (Platform Technology) are SDC-only — they don't exist in canonical ontology.
governance_findings.governance_action: Both REJECTED. Capabilities in non-canonical domains have no governance standing.
governance_findings.remediation: SDC domain derivation should be constrained to canonical CSR domain count or explicitly flag novel domains.

governance_findings.description: Two WEAKLY_GROUNDED domains (DOMAIN-02, DOMAIN-10) assigned 0.85 confidence identical to GROUNDED domains. The proposition bridge used uniform confidence for all domain grounding propositions regardless of grounding status.
governance_findings.governance_action: Both CONTESTED then ACCEPTED with confidence reduced to 0.65.
governance_findings.remediation: proposition_bridge.py should scale confidence based on grounding_status.

governance_findings.description: Four component duplications (Predictive Maintenance, Driver Scoring, Anomaly Detection, Digital Twin) classified as CROSS_DOMAIN_EVIDENCE but are functional overlaps within AI/ML capability space. No domain assignment — they don't demonstrate cross-domain relationships.
governance_findings.remediation: Cross-domain propositions from review queue should require domain assignment to qualify.

key_friction_sources: Domain ID mismatch between canonical CSR and SDC candidate CSR
SDC layer label parsing artifacts promoted to capabilities
SDC domain overflow beyond canonical 17-domain ontology
Uniform confidence assignment ignoring grounding status
Unassigned-domain components classified as cross-domain evidence
